title: Allowing command execution is security sensitive
description: Allowing command execution is security sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilitysecuritySecurity HotspotAllowing command execution (exec) for roles in a Kubernetes cluster can pose a significant security risk. This is because it provides the user with the ability to execute arbitrary commands within a container, potentially leading to unauthorized access or data breaches.In a production Kubernetes cluster, exec permissions are typically unnecessary due to the principle of least privilege, which suggests that a user or process should only have the minimum permissions necessary to perform its function. Additionally, containers in production are often treated as immutable infrastructure, meaning they should not be changed once deployed.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Allowing 'exec' verb in Kubernetes RBAC is security-sensitive
Shell command execution is security-sensitive - ensure proper input validation
Command execution is security-sensitive - ensure proper input validation

Kubernetes resource configuration - verify RBAC verb permissions do not include 'exec'
Kubernetes Role/ClusterRole - verify 'exec' verb is not allowed
Wildcard verbs '*' detected - this includes 'exec' permission
